title: Go SDK: Initialize Go module and project structure
description:
# Go SDK: Initialize Go module and project structure

Set up the Go module at `sdks/go/` with the initial directory layout and `go.mod`.

## Requirements

- Create `sdks/go/go.mod` with module path `github.com/clockworklabs/spacetimedb-go` (or appropriate org path)
- Require Go 1.21+ minimum (needed for generics, `slices`, `maps`, `iter` packages)
- Create package directory structure:
  ```
  sdks/go/
    go.mod
    go.sum
    bsatn/          # BSATN codec
    types/          # Special SpacetimeDB types (Identity, ConnectionId, etc.)
    protocol/       # WebSocket v2 message types
    client/         # Connection, cache, subscriptions, callbacks
    sdk.go          # Top-level package re-exports
  ```
- Add a basic `sdk.go` with package doc comment explaining this is the SpacetimeDB Go client SDK
- Add `.gitignore` entries if needed for Go build artifacts

## Acceptance Criteria

- `go build ./...` succeeds from `sdks/go/`
- Directory structure matches the layout above
- `go.mod` has correct module path and Go version

title: Go SDK: DbConnectionBuilder
description:
# Go SDK: DbConnectionBuilder

Implement the builder pattern for establishing database connections in `sdks/go/client/connection_builder.go`.

## API Design

```go
type DbConnectionBuilder struct {
    host         string
    moduleName   string
    token        string
    connectionId *types.ConnectionId
    compression  protocol.Compression
    onConnect    func(ctx EventContext, identity types.Identity, token string)
    onDisconnect func(ctx ErrorContext, err error)
    onConnectErr func(ctx ErrorContext, err error)
}

func NewDbConnectionBuilder() *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) WithUri(uri string) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) WithModuleName(name string) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) WithToken(token string) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) WithConnectionId(id types.ConnectionId) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) WithCompression(c protocol.Compression) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) OnConnect(fn func(EventContext, types.Identity, string)) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) OnDisconnect(fn func(ErrorContext, error)) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) OnConnectError(fn func(ErrorContext, error)) *DbConnectionBuilder
func (b *DbConnectionBuilder) Build() (*DbConnection, error)
```

## Build() behavior

1. Assemble WebSocket URL: `ws(s)://{host}/v1/database/{moduleName}/subscribe?compression={compression}`
2. If `connectionId` is set, add `&connection_id={hex}`
3. Set headers: `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: v2.bsatn.spacetimedb`, `Authorization: Bearer {token}`
4. Call `WsConnection.Dial()`
5. Wait for `InitialConnection` server message
6. Create `DbConnection` with identity, connection_id, token from server
7. Fire `onConnect` callback
8. Return the `DbConnection`

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/db_connection.rs` (DbConnectionBuilder)
- TypeScript: `crates/bindings-typescript/src/sdk/db_connection_builder.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Builder pattern with fluent API
- Validates required fields (host, moduleName)
- Successfully connects and receives InitialConnection
- Fires callbacks appropriately
- Returns meaningful errors on connection failure

title: Go SDK: Client cache (in-memory row store)
description:
# Go SDK: Client cache (in-memory row store)

Implement the client-side in-memory materialized view that mirrors subscribed table data, in `sdks/go/client/cache.go`.

## Design

The client cache stores rows for each subscribed table. Rows are keyed by their raw BSATN bytes (to provide Hash/Eq semantics even for types like floats). Reference counting handles overlapping subscription queries.

```go
type TableCache[Row any] struct {
    mu       sync.RWMutex
    entries  map[string]*rowEntry[Row]   // key = hex(bsatn bytes)
    decode   func(*bsatn.Reader) (Row, error)
}

type rowEntry[Row any] struct {
    row      Row
    bsatn    []byte
    refCount uint32
}

func NewTableCache[Row any](decode func(*bsatn.Reader) (Row, error)) *TableCache[Row]

// Query
func (c *TableCache[Row]) Count() int
func (c *TableCache[Row]) Iter() iter.Seq[Row]

// Mutations (called during message processing)
func (c *TableCache[Row]) ApplyInserts(rowsData []byte, sizeHint RowSizeHint) (inserted []Row, err error)
func (c *TableCache[Row]) ApplyDeletes(rowsData []byte, sizeHint RowSizeHint) (deleted []Row, err error)
```

## Unique Index Support

```go
type UniqueIndex[Row any, Col comparable] struct {
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    entries map[Col]*Row
    extract func(*Row) Col
}

func NewUniqueIndex[Row any, Col comparable](extract func(*Row) Col) *UniqueIndex[Row, Col]
func (idx *UniqueIndex[Row, Col]) Find(key Col) (*Row, bool)
func (idx *UniqueIndex[Row, Col]) insert(row *Row)
func (idx *UniqueIndex[Row, Col]) remove(row *Row)
```

## Reference Counting

When multiple subscriptions overlap on the same rows, ref counts track how many subscriptions "own" each row. A row is only truly deleted when its ref count reaches zero.

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/client_cache.rs`
- TypeScript: `crates/bindings-typescript/src/sdk/client_cache.ts`
- C#: `sdks/csharp/src/SpacetimeDB/ClientCache.cs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Insert/delete operations work correctly
- Ref counting prevents premature deletion of shared rows
- Unique index lookups work
- Thread-safe (multiple goroutines can read concurrently)
- Round-trip test: insert rows, verify Iter() returns them, delete rows, verify empty

title: Go SDK: Callback registry
description:
# Go SDK: Callback registry

Implement a type-safe callback system for table events and reducer results in `sdks/go/client/callbacks.go`.

## Design

```go
type CallbackId uint64

type CallbackRegistry[Ctx any, Args any] struct {
    mu      sync.Mutex
    nextId  CallbackId
    entries map[CallbackId]func(Ctx, Args)
}

func NewCallbackRegistry[Ctx any, Args any]() *CallbackRegistry[Ctx, Args]
func (r *CallbackRegistry[Ctx, Args]) Register(fn func(Ctx, Args)) CallbackId
func (r *CallbackRegistry[Ctx, Args]) Remove(id CallbackId)
func (r *CallbackRegistry[Ctx, Args]) Invoke(ctx Ctx, args Args)
```

## Table Callbacks

For each table, codegen will create:
- `onInsert` callbacks: `func(ctx *EventContext, row *Row)`
- `onDelete` callbacks: `func(ctx *EventContext, row *Row)`
- `onUpdate` callbacks (tables with PK): `func(ctx *EventContext, oldRow *Row, newRow *Row)`

Update detection: when a TransactionUpdate contains both a delete and insert for the same primary key, it's an update.

## Reducer Callbacks

For each reducer, codegen will create:
- `onReducer` callbacks: `func(ctx *ReducerEventContext, args ...)`

## Connection Callbacks

- `onConnect`: fired after InitialConnection received
- `onDisconnect`: fired when connection closes
- `onConnectError`: fired on connection failure

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/callbacks.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Generic callback registry with register/remove/invoke
- Thread-safe (concurrent register/invoke from different goroutines)
- CallbackId is unique and monotonically increasing
- Remove actually prevents future invocations

title: Go SDK: Subscription manager
description:
# Go SDK: Subscription manager

Implement subscription lifecycle management in `sdks/go/client/subscription.go`.

## Subscription State Machine

```
Pending → Sent → Applied → Ended
                ↘ Error
```

## API

```go
type SubscriptionBuilder struct {
    conn      *DbConnection
    onApplied func(*SubscriptionEventContext)
    onError   func(*ErrorContext, error)
}

func (b *SubscriptionBuilder) OnApplied(fn func(*SubscriptionEventContext)) *SubscriptionBuilder
func (b *SubscriptionBuilder) OnError(fn func(*ErrorContext, error)) *SubscriptionBuilder
func (b *SubscriptionBuilder) Subscribe(queries []string) *SubscriptionHandle
func (b *SubscriptionBuilder) SubscribeToAllTables() *SubscriptionHandle

type SubscriptionHandle struct {
    querySetId uint32
    state      atomic.Int32  // Pending=0, Sent=1, Applied=2, Ended=3, Error=4
}

func (h *SubscriptionHandle) IsActive() bool
func (h *SubscriptionHandle) IsEnded() bool
func (h *SubscriptionHandle) Unsubscribe()
func (h *SubscriptionHandle) UnsubscribeThen(onEnded func(*SubscriptionEventContext))
```

## Internal: SubscriptionManager

```go
type SubscriptionManager struct {
    mu           sync.Mutex
    nextQuerySet uint32
    active       map[uint32]*SubscriptionHandle
    pending      map[uint32]*SubscriptionHandle
}

func (m *SubscriptionManager) NewSubscription(queries []string, conn *WsConnection) *SubscriptionHandle
func (m *SubscriptionManager) HandleApplied(msg protocol.SubscribeApplied) // transitions Sent→Applied
func (m *SubscriptionManager) HandleError(msg protocol.SubscriptionError)   // transitions to Error
func (m *SubscriptionManager) HandleUnsubApplied(msg protocol.UnsubscribeApplied)
```

## Behavior

- `Subscribe()` sends a `ClientMessage::Subscribe` with a new `query_set_id`
- On `SubscribeApplied`: apply initial rows to client cache, fire `onApplied` callback
- On `SubscriptionError`: fire `onError` callback
- On `Unsubscribe()`: sends `ClientMessage::Unsubscribe`, transitions to Ended on `UnsubscribeApplied`
- `SubscribeToAllTables()`: uses special query `"SELECT * FROM *"` (verify exact syntax)

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/subscription.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- State machine transitions correctly
- Callbacks fire at the right times
- Multiple concurrent subscriptions work independently
- Unsubscribe sends the correct protocol message
- Thread-safe

title: Go SDK: DbConnection and message processing loop
description:
# Go SDK: DbConnection and message processing loop

Implement the main DbConnection type that ties together the WebSocket, cache, subscriptions, and callbacks in `sdks/go/client/db_connection.go`.

## Core Type

```go
type DbConnection struct {
    ws            *WsConnection
    identity      types.Identity
    connectionId  types.ConnectionId
    token         string
    cache         *ClientCacheManager
    subscriptions *SubscriptionManager
    callbacks     *DbCallbacks
    requestId     atomic.Uint32
    active        atomic.Bool
}
```

## Message Processing

The Go SDK should use an explicit pump model (like the Rust SDK) rather than automatic background processing, giving users control over when callbacks fire.

```go
// Process one incoming message. Blocks until a message is available or context is cancelled.
func (c *DbConnection) AdvanceOneMessage(ctx context.Context) error

// Process all available messages without blocking.
func (c *DbConnection) FrameTick() error

// Run the message loop until disconnected. Blocks the calling goroutine.
func (c *DbConnection) RunBlocking(ctx context.Context) error

// Run the message loop in a background goroutine.
func (c *DbConnection) RunAsync(ctx context.Context) <-chan error
```

## Message Handling

For each ServerMessage variant:

| Message | Action |
|---------|--------|
| InitialConnection | Store identity/connectionId/token, fire onConnect |
| SubscribeApplied | Apply initial rows to cache, fire subscription onApplied |
| UnsubscribeApplied | Remove rows from cache if provided, fire subscription onEnded |
| SubscriptionError | Fire subscription onError |
| TransactionUpdate | Apply inserts/deletes to cache, detect updates (delete+insert same PK), fire row callbacks |
| ReducerResult | Fire reducer callback with outcome |
| OneOffQueryResult | Fire one-off query callback |
| ProcedureResult | Fire procedure callback |

## Reducer Calls

```go
func (c *DbConnection) CallReducer(reducerName string, args []byte) (uint32, error)
```

Returns the request_id so the caller can correlate with ReducerResult.

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/db_connection.rs`
- TypeScript: `crates/bindings-typescript/src/sdk/db_connection_impl.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- All ServerMessage types handled correctly
- Cache updates trigger appropriate callbacks
- Update detection (delete+insert same PK) works
- RunBlocking/RunAsync/FrameTick all function correctly
- Clean shutdown via context cancellation
- Request IDs are atomically incremented and unique

Go SDK: Token/credential storage
description:
# Go SDK: Token/credential storage

Implement local storage for authentication tokens in `sdks/go/client/credentials.go`.

## Behavior

After initial connection, the server returns a token. The SDK should persist this token locally so reconnections can use the same identity.

```go
// SaveToken writes the token to a local file.
// Default path: ~/.spacetimedb/go_client_tokens/{host}_{module_name}
func SaveToken(host, moduleName, token string) error

// LoadToken reads a previously saved token.
func LoadToken(host, moduleName string) (string, error)

// TokenPath returns the file path where a token would be stored.
func TokenPath(host, moduleName string) string
```

## File Format

Plain text file containing just the token string (matching Rust SDK behavior).

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/credentials.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tokens are persisted to disk
- Tokens are loaded correctly on reconnect
- File permissions are restrictive (0600)
- Works on Linux, macOS, and Windows (use `os.UserHomeDir()`)

title: Go SDK: Codegen - implement Lang trait for Go
description:
# Go SDK: Codegen - implement Lang trait for Go

Add Go as a code generation target in `crates/codegen/src/go.rs`. This is a Rust file that implements the `Lang` trait to emit Go source files from module schema definitions.

## The Lang Trait

```rust
pub trait Lang {
    fn generate_type_files(&self, module: &ModuleDef, typ: &TypeDef) -> Vec<OutputFile>;
    fn generate_table_file_from_schema(&self, module: &ModuleDef, tbl: &TableDef, schema: TableSchema) -> OutputFile;
    fn generate_reducer_file(&self, module: &ModuleDef, reducer: &ReducerDef) -> OutputFile;
    fn generate_procedure_file(&self, module: &ModuleDef, procedure: &ProcedureDef) -> OutputFile;
    fn generate_global_files(&self, module: &ModuleDef, options: &CodegenOptions) -> Vec<OutputFile>;
}
```

## What to generate

### Type files (one per named type)

For each struct/enum in the module schema, emit a `.go` file with:
- Go struct/enum type definition
- `ReadXxx(r *bsatn.Reader) (Xxx, error)` function
- `func (v Xxx) WriteBsatn(w *bsatn.Writer)` method
- SATS type → Go type mapping:
  - Bool → bool, U8 → uint8, I8 → int8, U16 → uint16, ..., U64 → uint64, I64 → int64
  - U128 → types.U128, I128 → types.I128, U256 → types.U256, I256 → types.I256
  - F32 → float32, F64 → float64
  - String → string
  - Array → []T
  - Option → *T (nil = None)
  - Sum → Go interface with variant structs
  - Identity → types.Identity, ConnectionId → types.ConnectionId
  - Timestamp → types.Timestamp, TimeDuration → types.TimeDuration

### Table files (one per table)

For each table, emit:
- `XxxTableHandle` struct wrapping `*client.TableCache[Xxx]`
- `Count()`, `Iter()` methods
- `OnInsert()`, `OnDelete()`, `RemoveOnInsert()`, `RemoveOnDelete()` callback methods
- If table has a primary key: `OnUpdate()`, `RemoveOnUpdate()`, `FindByPk()` methods
- `FilterByXxx()` for each indexed column

### Reducer files (one per reducer)

For each reducer, emit:
- Typed method on `RemoteReducers`: `func (r *RemoteReducers) MyReducer(arg1 Type1, ...) error`
- Args struct with BSATN serialization
- `OnMyReducer()` callback registration

### Procedure files (one per procedure)

Same pattern as reducers but for procedures.

### Global module file

Emit a single `module_bindings.go` containing:
- `RemoteTables` struct with accessor methods for each table
- `RemoteReducers` struct
- `RemoteProcedures` struct
- `DbConnection` type alias/wrapper
- `EventContext`, `ReducerEventContext`, `SubscriptionEventContext`, `ErrorContext` types
- `Reducer` interface and per-reducer args types
- `DbUpdate` / `AppliedDiff` types for transaction processing
- `RegisterTables()` function

## Go-specific considerations

- Use `gofmt`-compatible output (or run `gofmt` post-generation)
- Package name should match the output directory
- Import paths must be correct for the SDK module
- Use Go naming conventions (exported = PascalCase, unexported = camelCase)

## Reference

- Existing codegen implementations:
  - Rust: `crates/codegen/src/rust.rs`
  - TypeScript: `crates/codegen/src/typescript.rs`
  - C#: `crates/codegen/src/csharp.rs`
  - C++: `crates/codegen/src/cpp.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Lang` trait fully implemented for Go
- Generated Go code compiles with `go build`
- Generated BSATN read/write round-trips correctly
- Generated code follows Go naming conventions
- All SATS types map to appropriate Go types

title: Go SDK: Wire codegen into CLI (spacetime generate --lang go)
description:
# Go SDK: Wire codegen into CLI (spacetime generate --lang go)

Register Go as a language option in the `spacetime generate` CLI command so users can generate Go client bindings.

## Changes needed

1. **`crates/codegen/src/lib.rs`**: Add `pub mod go;` and `pub use self::go::Go;`
2. **`crates/cli/`**: Find where language selection happens for `spacetime generate` and add `"go" | "golang"` as an option that instantiates `codegen::Go`
3. Update help text/docs to list Go as a supported language

## CLI Usage

```bash
spacetime generate --lang go --out-dir ./client --module-name my_module
```

## Reference

- Grep for how other languages are wired in: search for `"typescript"`, `"csharp"`, `"rust"` in `crates/cli/`
- `crates/codegen/src/lib.rs` for the `generate()` entry point

## Acceptance Criteria

- `spacetime generate --lang go` produces Go files
- `spacetime generate --help` lists `go` as a valid language
- Generated Go code compiles when placed in a Go module that imports the Go SDK

title: Go SDK: SATS type mapping tests (codegen verification)
description:
# Go SDK: SATS type mapping tests (codegen verification)

Create a comprehensive test module and verify that Go codegen produces correct, compilable, BSATN-compatible code for all SATS types.

## Test Module

Create a test module (Rust WASM) at `modules/sdk-test-go/` (or reuse `modules/sdk-test/`) that defines tables and reducers exercising every SATS type:

- All primitive types: bool, u8, i8, u16, i16, u32, i32, u64, i64, u128, i128, u256, i256, f32, f64
- String
- Arrays (Vec<T>)
- Option<T> (nullable fields)
- Nested structs (Product in Product)
- Enums (Sum types with named variants)
- Special types: Identity, ConnectionId, Timestamp, Uuid
- Tables with various index types (unique, non-unique)
- Reducers with multiple argument types

## Verification Steps

1. Publish the test module to a local SpacetimeDB instance
2. Run `spacetime generate --lang go` against it
3. Verify the generated Go code compiles (`go build ./...`)
4. Write Go tests that:
   - BSATN-encode values using the generated code
   - Compare encoded bytes against known-good values from Rust BSATN encoding
   - Decode known-good BSATN bytes and verify the resulting Go values

## Reference

- Existing SDK tests: `modules/sdk-test/` (the standard test module)
- Rust SDK tests: `sdks/rust/tests/`
- TypeScript SDK tests: `crates/bindings-typescript/test-app/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- All SATS types produce valid Go code
- Generated code compiles
- BSATN encoding matches Rust reference encoding byte-for-byte
- BSATN decoding of Rust-produced bytes works correctly

title: Go SDK: BSATN Writer (encoder)
Go SDK: Integration test - connect, subscribe, call reducer
description:
# Go SDK: Integration test - connect, subscribe, call reducer

Create an end-to-end integration test that connects to a SpacetimeDB instance, subscribes to tables, calls a reducer, and verifies the client cache updates.

## Test Plan

1. Start a local SpacetimeDB standalone instance
2. Publish a simple test module with:
   - A `person` table: `{ id: u64, name: String }`
   - An `add_person` reducer: inserts a row
   - A `delete_person` reducer: deletes by id
3. Generate Go client bindings
4. In Go test:
   - Connect via `DbConnectionBuilder`
   - Subscribe to `SELECT * FROM person`
   - Wait for `SubscribeApplied` (initially empty)
   - Call `add_person(1, "Alice")`
   - Wait for `TransactionUpdate` with insert
   - Verify `person` table cache has 1 row
   - Verify `OnInsert` callback fired with correct data
   - Call `add_person(2, "Bob")`
   - Verify 2 rows in cache
   - Call `delete_person(1)`
   - Verify `OnDelete` callback fired
   - Verify 1 row remaining
   - Disconnect cleanly

## Test Location

`sdks/go/tests/integration_test.go` or as a separate test module.

## Reference

- Rust SDK integration tests: `sdks/rust/tests/`
- TypeScript integration tests: `crates/bindings-typescript/test-app/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Full round-trip: connect → subscribe → reducer call → cache update → callback
- Test passes against a real SpacetimeDB instance
- Clean connection shutdown

title: Go SDK: One-off query support
description:
# Go SDK: One-off query support

Implement one-off SQL query execution (non-subscription queries) in the Go client SDK.

## API

```go
// OneOffQuery executes a SQL query and returns the results without creating a subscription.
func (c *DbConnection) OneOffQuery(ctx context.Context, query string) (*QueryResult, error)

type QueryResult struct {
    Tables []TableRows
}

type TableRows struct {
    TableName string
    Rows      [][]byte  // raw BSATN rows
}
```

## Protocol

1. Send `ClientMessage::OneOffQuery { request_id, query_string }`
2. Wait for `ServerMessage::OneOffQueryResult { request_id, result }` with matching request_id
3. On `Ok(QueryRows)`: decode and return
4. On `Err(string)`: return error

## Implementation Notes

- Use a map of `request_id → chan<- *QueryResult` to route responses to the correct caller
- Support context cancellation for timeout

## Reference

- Protocol: `crates/client-api-messages/src/websocket/v2.rs` (OneOffQuery/OneOffQueryResult)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Can execute arbitrary SQL and get results
- Request/response correlation works with concurrent queries
- Timeout via context works
- Error responses (bad SQL) are properly returned

title: Go SDK: Procedure call support
description:
# Go SDK: Procedure call support

Implement procedure call support in the Go client SDK. Procedures are like reducers but return a value and don't produce transaction updates.

## API

```go
func (c *DbConnection) CallProcedure(ctx context.Context, procedureName string, args []byte) ([]byte, error)
```

## Protocol

1. Send `ClientMessage::CallProcedure { request_id, flags: 0, procedure, args }`
2. Wait for `ServerMessage::ProcedureResult { request_id, timestamp, total_host_execution_duration, status }`
3. On `Returned(bytes)`: return the bytes
4. On `InternalError(string)`: return error

## Implementation Notes

- Same request_id correlation pattern as OneOffQuery (STDB-21)
- Codegen (STDB-17) will produce typed wrappers per procedure

## Reference

- Protocol: `crates/client-api-messages/src/websocket/v2.rs` (CallProcedure/ProcedureResult)
- Rust SDK: procedure handling in `sdks/rust/src/db_connection.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Can call procedures and receive return values
- Error handling for InternalError status
- Works with codegen-produced typed wrappers

title: Go Server SDK: Evaluate WASM compilation strategy for Go modules
description:
# Go Server SDK: Evaluate WASM compilation strategy for Go modules

Research and decide how Go code will compile to WebAssembly for execution inside SpacetimeDB's Wasmtime runtime.

## Background

SpacetimeDB hosts modules as `wasm32-unknown-unknown` WebAssembly binaries. The WASM ABI is defined by the `spacetime_10.0` import module (see `crates/bindings-sys/`).

## Research Findings

### Key Finding: No WASI Support

Inspecting `crates/core/src/host/wasmtime/wasmtime_module.rs` confirms that SpacetimeDB's Wasmtime host **does NOT support WASI**. It only provides the custom `spacetime_10.x` import module (versions 10.0 through 10.4 per `crates/bindings-sys/src/lib.rs`).

This eliminates:
- **Option B** (Standard Go WASI / `GOOS=wasip1`) — requires WASI host support
- **Option C** (js/wasm) — browser-only, not suitable for Wasmtime

### Decision: TinyGo

**Chosen approach: TinyGo targeting `wasm` (bare metal wasm32)**

**Rationale:**
1. TinyGo can produce `wasm32-unknown-unknown` binaries (via `GOOS=wasip1 GOARCH=wasm` or custom target file)
2. TinyGo supports importing from custom WASM modules via `//go:linkname` and linker flags
3. TinyGo is the only Go compiler that can produce sufficiently small binaries for module hosting
4. The `spacetime_10.x` ABI uses simple linear memory and function imports — compatible with TinyGo's memory model
5. The limited stdlib (no full reflect, no cgo) is acceptable for server-side modules which should avoid heavy stdlib dependencies

### Implementation Approach

Go server modules will use TinyGo with a custom target file:
- Target: `wasm32-unknown-unknown` (not wasip1)
- Import the `spacetime_10.0` module functions via `//go:linkname` declarations
- Export module init/reducers via `//export` pragmas
- Memory: use `malloc`/`free` compatible with SpacetimeDB's expected memory ABI

### Required TinyGo Constraints

- No goroutines in module callbacks (SpacetimeDB is single-threaded per reducer invocation)
- No stdlib packages that require OS or network (acceptable for pure logic modules)
- Linear memory allocator is sufficient

## Acceptance Criteria

- ✅ Decision documented with rationale
- STDB-24: Implement the TinyGo-based bindings
- STDB-25: Create build tooling for Go→WASM compilation
- STDB-29: Write a hello-world Go module that compiles and runs in SpacetimeDB

title: Go Server SDK: WASM FFI bindings (spacetime_10.0 ABI)
description:
# Go Server SDK: WASM FFI bindings (spacetime_10.0 ABI)

Implement the raw FFI layer that Go modules use to communicate with the SpacetimeDB host via the `spacetime_10.0` WASM import module.

## ABI Functions to Import

These are the functions the WASM host provides (from `crates/bindings-sys/src/lib.rs`):

```
// Table operations
table_id_from_name(name_ptr: u32, name_len: u32, out: *mut u32) -> u16
datastore_table_scan_bsatn(table_id: u32, out: *mut u32) -> u16
datastore_insert_bsatn(table_id: u32, row_ptr: u32, row_len: u32, out_len: *mut u32) -> u16
datastore_delete_by_btree_scan_bsatn(table_id: u32, index_id: u32, prefix_ptr: u32, prefix_len: u32, rstart_ptr: u32, rstart_len: u32, rend_ptr: u32, rend_len: u32, out: *mut u32) -> u16

// Index operations
datastore_index_scan_range_bsatn(index_id: u32, prefix_ptr: u32, prefix_len: u32, rstart_ptr: u32, rstart_len: u32, rend_ptr: u32, rend_len: u32, out: *mut u32) -> u16

// Iterator operations
row_iter_bsatn_advance(iter_id: u32, buf_ptr: u32, buf_len: *mut u32) -> i16
row_iter_bsatn_close(iter_id: u32)

// Buffer operations
bytes_source_read(source_id: u32, buf_ptr: u32, buf_len: *mut u32) -> i16
bytes_sink_write(sink_id: u32, buf_ptr: u32, buf_len: u32)

// Scheduling
schedule_reducer(name_ptr: u32, name_len: u32, args_ptr: u32, args_len: u32, time: u64, out: *mut u64) -> u16
cancel_reducer(schedule_id: u64)

// Logging
console_log(level: u8, msg_ptr: u32, msg_len: u32)

// Identity
connection_id(out: *mut u8)
identity(out: *mut u8)
timestamp(out: *mut u64)

// Module description
volatile_nonatomic_schedule_immediate_bsatn(name_ptr: u32, name_len: u32, args_ptr: u32, args_len: u32)
```

## Exports the Module Must Provide

```
__describe_module__() -> (ptr: u32, len: u32)  // returns RawModuleDef BSATN
__call_reducer__(id: u32, sender_0..3: u64, conn_0..1: u64, timestamp: u64, args: u32, args_len: u32) -> i16
```

## Implementation Strategy

This depends heavily on the WASM compilation decision (STDB-23):
- **TinyGo**: Use `//go:wasmimport spacetime_10.0 function_name` directives (TinyGo 0.30+)
- **Standard Go WASI**: May need a different approach via WASI + adapter

Create `sdks/go/server/sys/` with the raw FFI wrappers.

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/bindings-sys/src/lib.rs`
- C++: `crates/bindings-cpp/src/stdb_common.h`

## Acceptance Criteria

- All required host functions are importable from Go WASM
- Exports `__describe_module__` and `__call_reducer__` correctly
- Basic smoke test: a Go WASM module that the SpacetimeDB host can load and call `__describe_module__`

title: Go Server SDK: BSATN codec (server-side, WASM-compatible)
description:
# Go Server SDK: BSATN codec (server-side, WASM-compatible)

Implement BSATN serialization for the server-side Go module SDK. This may share code with the client SDK (STDB-2, STDB-3) or be a separate implementation depending on the WASM compilation constraints.

## Key Difference from Client SDK

The server-side BSATN codec operates inside WASM linear memory. It reads/writes from byte buffers that may be passed directly to/from host FFI functions. The codec must:

- Work within WASM's linear memory model
- Be compatible with TinyGo or whichever compiler is chosen (STDB-23)
- Avoid features not available in the chosen compiler (e.g., TinyGo has no `reflect` package)

## Options

1. **Share code**: If the client SDK's BSATN package compiles under TinyGo/WASI, import it directly
2. **Fork**: If compiler limitations prevent sharing, create a minimal BSATN codec tailored for WASM

## Implementation Location

`sdks/go/bsatn/` (shared from the client SDK — TinyGo-compatible)

## Acceptance Criteria

- BSATN Reader/Writer compile and run inside WASM
- Byte-compatible with the client SDK and Rust BSATN
- Performance is acceptable for in-module use

title: Go Server SDK: ReducerContext and high-level table API
description:
# Go Server SDK: ReducerContext and high-level table API

Implement the high-level Go API that module authors use to define tables, access data, and write reducers.

## ReducerContext

```go
// ReducerContext is passed to every reducer function.
type ReducerContext struct {
    Db           *DbContext
    Identity     types.Identity
    ConnectionId types.ConnectionId
    Timestamp    types.Timestamp
}
```

## DbContext (table access)

```go
type DbContext struct {
    // Generated per-module: one field per table
}

// For each table, codegen generates:
type MyTableAccessor struct{}

func (a *MyTableAccessor) Insert(row MyRow) error
func (a *MyTableAccessor) Iter() iter.Seq[MyRow]
func (a *MyTableAccessor) FilterByColumn(val ColType) iter.Seq[MyRow]
func (a *MyTableAccessor) FindByUniqueCol(val ColType) (*MyRow, bool)
func (a *MyTableAccessor) DeleteByUniqueCol(val ColType) (bool, error)
func (a *MyTableAccessor) UpdateByUniqueCol(val ColType, newRow MyRow) error
```

## Under the Hood

Each method calls into the FFI layer (STDB-24):
- `Insert` → BSATN-encode the row → `datastore_insert_bsatn`
- `Iter` → `datastore_table_scan_bsatn` → iterate with `row_iter_bsatn_advance`
- `FilterByColumn` → `datastore_index_scan_range_bsatn` with appropriate prefix

## Logging

```go
func Log(level LogLevel, msg string)  // calls console_log FFI
```

Log levels: Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error, Panic

## Scheduling

```go
func (ctx *ReducerContext) ScheduleReducer(name string, args []byte, at types.Timestamp) (uint64, error)
func (ctx *ReducerContext) CancelReducer(scheduleId uint64)
```

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/bindings/src/lib.rs`, `crates/bindings/src/table.rs`
- C#: `crates/bindings-csharp/Runtime/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- ReducerContext provides identity, connection_id, timestamp
- Table Insert/Iter/Filter/Find/Delete/Update all work via FFI
- Logging works
- Scheduling works
- Error handling: reducer panics are caught and result in transaction rollback

title: Go Server SDK: Module descriptor (__describe_module__)
description:
# Go Server SDK: Module descriptor (__describe_module__)

Implement the `__describe_module__` WASM export that returns the module's schema (RawModuleDef) as BSATN bytes.

## What __describe_module__ does

When SpacetimeDB loads a WASM module, it calls `__describe_module__()` which returns a pointer and length to a BSATN-encoded `RawModuleDefV10` describing:

- All tables: name, columns (name + SATS type), constraints, indexes, visibility (public/private), scheduled reducer
- All reducers: name, parameter types
- All types: named struct/enum definitions
- Module typespace: the `Typespace` containing all referenced `AlgebraicType`s

## Implementation Strategy

Go modules need a way to register their tables/reducers at init time. Two approaches:

### Option A: Code generation (recommended)
A Go-specific codegen tool (or extension of STDB-17) that, given annotated Go source, generates:
- The `__describe_module__` export with hardcoded BSATN bytes
- Reducer dispatch in `__call_reducer__`

### Option B: Runtime registration
Go `init()` functions register tables/reducers into a global registry, which is serialized on `__describe_module__` call.

## RawModuleDefV10 Structure

```
RawModuleDefV10 {
    typespace: Typespace,
    tables: Vec<RawTableDefV9>,
    reducers: Vec<RawReducerDefV9>,
    types: Vec<RawTypeDefV9>,
    misc_exports: Vec<RawMiscModuleExport>,
    row_level_security: Vec<RawRowLevelSecurityDefV9>,
}
```

Each type is BSATN-encoded. See `crates/schema/src/def/` for the full definitions.

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/bindings-macro/` (generates `__describe_module__` contributions)
- C++: `crates/bindings-cpp/src/stdb_module.cpp` (runtime registration approach)
- Schema: `crates/schema/src/def/raw_def_v10.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `__describe_module__` returns valid RawModuleDefV10 BSATN
- SpacetimeDB host can parse the descriptor and create tables
- At least one table and one reducer are correctly described

title: Go Server SDK: Reducer dispatch (__call_reducer__)
description:
# Go Server SDK: Reducer dispatch (__call_reducer__)

Implement the `__call_reducer__` WASM export that dispatches incoming reducer calls to the correct Go function.

## ABI Signature

```
__call_reducer__(
    id: u32,              // reducer index (matches order in __describe_module__)
    sender_0: u64,        // identity bytes 0-7
    sender_1: u64,        // identity bytes 8-15
    sender_2: u64,        // (unused/reserved)
    sender_3: u64,        // (unused/reserved)
    conn_0: u64,          // connection_id bytes 0-7
    conn_1: u64,          // connection_id bytes 8-15
    timestamp: u64,       // microseconds since epoch
    args: u32,            // pointer to BSATN-encoded args
    args_len: u32         // length of args
) -> i16                  // 0 = success, -1 = error
```

## Implementation

1. Reconstruct `Identity` from sender_0..1 bytes
2. Reconstruct `ConnectionId` from conn_0..1 bytes
3. Build `ReducerContext` with identity, connection_id, timestamp
4. Read args from WASM linear memory at `[args..args+args_len]`
5. Look up reducer function by `id` (index into registered reducers)
6. BSATN-decode args according to the reducer's parameter types
7. Call the Go reducer function
8. Return 0 on success, -1 on panic/error

## Panic Recovery

Go reducers that panic should be caught with `recover()`. The host expects that on error, the transaction is rolled back. The error message should be written via `console_log` before returning -1.

## Special Reducers

Some reducers have special semantics (registered by convention):
- `__init__` — called once when the module is first published
- `__identity_connected__` — called when a client connects
- `__identity_disconnected__` — called when a client disconnects

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/bindings-sys/src/lib.rs` (raw_call_reducer)
- C++: `crates/bindings-cpp/src/stdb_module.cpp` (__call_reducer__)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly dispatches to the right reducer by id
- BSATN args are decoded and passed as typed Go parameters
- Panics are recovered and reported
- Special reducers (__init__, connect/disconnect) work
- Identity and ConnectionId are correctly reconstructed from u64 halves

title: Go Server SDK: Build tooling (compile Go modules to WASM)

# Go Server SDK: Build tooling (compile Go modules to WASM)

Create build tooling that compiles Go SpacetimeDB modules into WASM binaries suitable for publishing.

## Requirements

1. **Build command**: Either a standalone tool or integration into `spacetime publish`
2. **Compiler invocation**: Based on the decision in STDB-23:
   - TinyGo: `tinygo build -o module.wasm -target=wasi ./`
   - Standard Go: `GOOS=wasip1 GOARCH=wasm go build -o module.wasm ./`
3. **WASM post-processing**: May need `wasm-opt` or adapter conversion depending on target
4. **Template**: Create a Go module template at `templates/basic-go/` for `spacetime init --lang go`

## CLI Integration

Update `crates/cli/` to support Go modules:
- `spacetime init --lang go` — scaffolds a new Go module project
- `spacetime publish` — detect Go project (by `go.mod`), compile to WASM, upload
- `spacetime build` — compile Go to WASM locally

## Template Structure

```
templates/basic-go/
  go.mod
  main.go          # Example module with a table and reducer
  spacetimedb.toml # Module config (if needed)
```

## Reference

- Rust template: `templates/basic-rs/`
- C# compilation: grep for how C# modules are compiled in `crates/cli/`
- C++ compilation: `crates/bindings-cpp/` build scripts

## Acceptance Criteria

- `spacetime init --lang go` creates a working Go module project
- `spacetime build` compiles it to WASM
- `spacetime publish` can upload the resulting WASM to a running SpacetimeDB instance
- The published module loads and responds to `__describe_module__`

Go Server SDK: Server-side codegen (table/reducer annotations)
description:
# Go Server SDK: Server-side codegen (table/reducer annotations)

Create a code generation tool or pattern that lets Go module authors declare tables and reducers with minimal boilerplate, and generates the glue code (`__describe_module__`, `__call_reducer__` dispatch, BSATN serialization).

## Background

Rust uses proc macros (`#[spacetimedb::table]`, `#[spacetimedb::reducer]`). Go doesn't have macros, so alternatives are needed.

## Options

### Option A: `go generate` tool
A standalone binary (e.g., `spacetimedb-go-gen`) that parses Go source files, finds annotated types/functions, and generates companion files.

Annotations via comments:
```go
//spacetimedb:table(name=person, public)
type Person struct {
    ID   uint64 `stdb:"primarykey"`
    Name string `stdb:"unique"`
}

//spacetimedb:reducer
func AddPerson(ctx *spacetimedb.ReducerContext, id uint64, name string) {
    ctx.Db.Person().Insert(Person{ID: id, Name: name})
}
```

Running `go generate` would produce:
- `zz_generated_person.go` — BSATN read/write, table accessor
- `zz_generated_reducers.go` — __call_reducer__ dispatch
- `zz_generated_module.go` — __describe_module__

### Option B: Builder pattern (runtime registration)
```go
func init() {
    spacetimedb.RegisterTable("person", PersonSchema())
    spacetimedb.RegisterReducer("add_person", AddPerson)
}
```
Less ergonomic but no codegen step needed. This is closer to the C++ approach.

### Recommendation

Option A is more idiomatic Go and provides better DX. The codegen tool should be written in Go and invoked via `//go:generate spacetimedb-go-gen`.

## Reference

- Rust macros: `crates/bindings-macro/`
- C# Roslyn source gen: `crates/bindings-csharp/BSATN.Codegen/`
- C++ macros: `crates/bindings-cpp/src/stdb_module.h`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Module authors can define tables and reducers with minimal ceremony
- Generated code handles BSATN serialization, module description, and reducer dispatch
- Works with `go generate` workflow
- Struct tags or comments provide table metadata (visibility, indexes, primary key)

title: Go Server SDK: Integration test - publish and run Go module
description:
# Go Server SDK: Integration test - publish and run Go module

End-to-end test that compiles a Go module, publishes it to SpacetimeDB, and verifies it works.

## Test Plan

1. Write a simple Go module with:
   - A `message` table: `{ sender: Identity, text: String, timestamp: Timestamp }`
   - A `send_message` reducer
   - An `__init__` reducer that inserts a welcome message
2. Compile to WASM using the build tooling (STDB-29)
3. Start a local SpacetimeDB instance
4. Publish the module via `spacetime publish`
5. Verify `__init__` ran (query for the welcome message)
6. Call `send_message` via `spacetime call`
7. Query the table via `spacetime sql` and verify the message exists
8. Connect a Go client (if STDB-20 is done) and verify subscription updates

## Verification

- Module loads without errors
- `__describe_module__` returns valid schema
- Reducers execute correctly
- Data persists across reducer calls
- Client subscriptions receive updates from Go module reducers

## Acceptance Criteria

- Full round-trip: write Go module → compile → publish → execute → query
- No WASM runtime errors
- Data integrity verified

title: Go SDK: Special SpacetimeDB types
description:
# Go SDK: Special SpacetimeDB types

Implement SpacetimeDB-specific types in `sdks/go/types/`. These are encoded specially in BSATN and recognized by their product tag in the SATS type system.

## Types to implement

### Identity (`types/identity.go`)
- 16 raw bytes (128-bit), encoded as `[16]byte` in BSATN
- SATS tag: `__identity__`
- Methods: `String()` (hex), `IsZero()`, `Bytes()`, `FromBytes([]byte)`

### ConnectionId (`types/connection_id.go`)
- 16 raw bytes (128-bit), encoded as `[16]byte`
- SATS tag: `__connection_id__`
- Same methods as Identity

### Timestamp (`types/timestamp.go`)
- Wire format: 1-field Product containing I64 (nanoseconds since Unix epoch)
- BSATN: just write/read an i64 (the product wrapper has one field, so it's just the field)
- Go type: `type Timestamp struct { Nanos int64 }`
- Methods: `ToTime() time.Time`, `FromTime(time.Time) Timestamp`, `String()`

### TimeDuration (`types/time_duration.go`)
- Wire format: 1-field Product containing I64 (nanoseconds)
- Go type: `type TimeDuration struct { Nanos int64 }`
- Methods: `ToDuration() time.Duration`, `FromDuration(time.Duration) TimeDuration`

### Uuid (`types/uuid.go`)
- 16 raw bytes
- Methods: `String()` (standard UUID format), `FromString(string)`, `Bytes()`

### I128/U128 (`types/int128.go`)
- `type U128 struct { Lo, Hi uint64 }` — LE: Lo is the lower 64 bits, written first
- `type I128 struct { Lo uint64; Hi int64 }` — signed variant
- BSATN: 16 bytes LE (Lo first, then Hi)
- Methods: `String()`, `IsZero()`, basic arithmetic not required but nice-to-have

### I256/U256 (`types/int256.go`)
- `type U256 [32]byte` — raw 32 bytes LE
- `type I256 [32]byte`
- Methods: `String()` (hex), `IsZero()`

## Each type must implement

- `WriteBsatn(w *bsatn.Writer)` for serialization
- `ReadXxx(r *bsatn.Reader) (Xxx, error)` function for deserialization
- `fmt.Stringer` interface

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/sats/src/` (identity.rs, connection_id.rs, timestamp.rs, etc.)
- C#: `crates/bindings-csharp/BSATN.Runtime/`

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 7 types implemented with BSATN read/write
- Round-trip tests for each type
- String representation tests

title: Go SDK: AlgebraicType runtime representation
Go SDK: WebSocket v2 protocol message types
description:
# Go SDK: WebSocket v2 protocol message types

Define all v2 WebSocket protocol message types and their BSATN serialization in `sdks/go/protocol/`.

## Client → Server Messages (`protocol/client_message.go`)

All are variants of a `ClientMessage` Sum type (u8 tag prefix):

| Tag | Variant | Fields |
|-----|---------|--------|
| 0 | Subscribe | request_id: u32, query_set_id: u32, query_strings: []string |
| 1 | Unsubscribe | request_id: u32, query_set_id: u32, flags: u8 (0=Default, 1=SendDroppedRows) |
| 2 | OneOffQuery | request_id: u32, query_string: string |
| 3 | CallReducer | request_id: u32, flags: u8, reducer: string, args: []byte |
| 4 | CallProcedure | request_id: u32, flags: u8, procedure: string, args: []byte |

Implement `WriteClientMessage(w *bsatn.Writer, msg ClientMessage)`.

## Server → Client Messages (`protocol/server_message.go`)

All are variants of a `ServerMessage` Sum type:

| Tag | Variant | Key Fields |
|-----|---------|------------|
| 0 | InitialConnection | identity: Identity, connection_id: ConnectionId, token: string |
| 1 | SubscribeApplied | request_id: u32, query_set_id: u32, rows: QueryRows |
| 2 | UnsubscribeApplied | request_id: u32, query_set_id: u32, rows: *QueryRows (Option) |
| 3 | SubscriptionError | request_id: *u32 (Option), query_set_id: u32, error: string |
| 4 | TransactionUpdate | query_sets: []QuerySetUpdate |
| 5 | OneOffQueryResult | request_id: u32, result: Result<QueryRows, string> |
| 6 | ReducerResult | request_id: u32, timestamp: i64, result: ReducerOutcome |
| 7 | ProcedureResult | request_id: u32, timestamp: i64, total_host_execution_duration: TimeDuration, status: ProcedureStatus |

Implement `ReadServerMessage(r *bsatn.Reader) (ServerMessage, error)`.

## Supporting Types (`protocol/types.go`)

```go
type QueryRows struct {
    Tables []SingleTableRows
}
type SingleTableRows struct {
    TableName string
    Rows      BsatnRowList
}
type BsatnRowList struct {
    SizeHint RowSizeHint
    RowsData []byte
}
type RowSizeHint interface{} // Sum: FixedSize(uint16) | RowOffsets([]uint64)

type QuerySetUpdate struct {
    QuerySetId uint32
    Tables     []TableUpdate
}
type TableUpdate struct {
    TableName string
    Rows      []TableUpdateRows
}
type TableUpdateRows interface{} // Sum: PersistentTable{inserts, deletes} | EventTable{events}

type ReducerOutcome interface{} // Sum: Ok{ret_value, tx_update} | OkEmpty | Err([]byte) | InternalError(string)
type ProcedureStatus interface{} // Sum: Returned([]byte) | InternalError(string)
```

## BsatnRowList Iterator

```go
func (l *BsatnRowList) IterRows() iter.Seq[[]byte]
```
- If FixedSize: each row is `size` bytes, slice sequentially
- If RowOffsets: use offsets to slice variable-length rows

## Reference

- Rust: `crates/client-api-messages/src/websocket/v2.rs`
- The Sum type tag values MUST match exactly — verify against the Rust source

## Acceptance Criteria

- All client and server message types defined
- BSATN encode for ClientMessage, decode for ServerMessage
- BsatnRowList iterator works for both FixedSize and RowOffsets
- Test with sample binary data captured from Rust serialization

title: Go SDK: Message compression (brotli/gzip)
description:
# Go SDK: Message compression (brotli/gzip)

Implement message decompression for server messages and compression preference negotiation in `sdks/go/protocol/compression.go`.

## Wire Format

Server messages arrive as binary WebSocket frames:
- **Byte 0**: compression tag
  - `0x00` = no compression
  - `0x01` = brotli
  - `0x02` = gzip
- **Bytes 1..N**: payload (compressed or raw BSATN)

Client messages are sent as raw BSATN without a compression prefix byte.

## Implementation

```go
type Compression uint8

const (
    CompressionNone   Compression = 0
    CompressionBrotli Compression = 1
    CompressionGzip   Compression = 2
)

func DecompressServerMessage(data []byte) ([]byte, error)
```

## Dependencies

- `compress/gzip` (stdlib) for gzip
- `github.com/andybalholm/brotli` (or similar) for brotli decompression

## Connection URL

The compression preference is communicated via query parameter on the WebSocket URL:
`ws://host/v1/database/{name}/subscribe?compression=Brotli`

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/compression.rs`
- TypeScript: `crates/bindings-typescript/src/sdk/decompress.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Decompress brotli, gzip, and no-compression server messages
- Error on unknown compression tag
- Test with pre-compressed payloads

title: Go SDK: WebSocket connection manager
description:
# Go SDK: WebSocket connection manager

Implement the WebSocket connection layer in `sdks/go/client/websocket.go`. This handles the raw WebSocket I/O, keepalive, and message framing.

## Connection URL Format

```
ws(s)://{host}/v1/database/{module_name}/subscribe?compression=Brotli&connection_id={id}&confirmed=true
```

Required HTTP headers:
- `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: v2.bsatn.spacetimedb`
- `Authorization: Bearer {token}` (if authenticated)

## Implementation

```go
type WsConnection struct {
    conn     *websocket.Conn
    incoming chan ParsedMessage
    outgoing chan []byte
    done     chan struct{}
}

type ParsedMessage struct {
    Message protocol.ServerMessage
    Err     error
}

func Dial(ctx context.Context, url string, headers http.Header) (*WsConnection, error)
func (ws *WsConnection) Send(msg protocol.ClientMessage) error
func (ws *WsConnection) Recv() <-chan ParsedMessage
func (ws *WsConnection) Close() error
```

## Background goroutines

1. **Read loop**: reads binary frames → decompresses → BSATN decodes to ServerMessage → sends to `incoming` channel
2. **Write loop**: reads from `outgoing` channel → BSATN encodes ClientMessage → sends as binary frame
3. **Keepalive**: sends WebSocket ping every 30 seconds of idle to detect dead connections

## Dependencies

Use `nhooyr.io/websocket` or `github.com/gorilla/websocket` for WebSocket support.

## Reference

- Rust SDK: `sdks/rust/src/websocket.rs`
- TypeScript: `crates/bindings-typescript/src/sdk/ws.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Can connect to a running SpacetimeDB instance
- Receives and decodes InitialConnection message
- Sends Subscribe/CallReducer messages successfully
- Keepalive ping works
- Clean shutdown on Close()
- Handles connection errors gracefully (closed by server, network timeout)
